我一直以为，如果贸易展览会灭绝，那将是一个渐进的过程-逐渐淡化为与文化无关的东西，例如书店和唱片店（我都非常想念这一切）。技术出于许多目的和目的，已大大降低了它们对我们社会的相对价值。
 毫无疑问，Spotify和Kindle Store缺乏现实世界中同类产品的吸引力和魅力，但我们很乐意为便利而牺牲一切，而更多。
  不断蔓延的大流行有效地使我们有了一年的不面对面的贸易展览。这意味着，除其他事项外，我们在有关展览会的问题中拥有一个更为直接的控制变量。去年的CES设法做到了。下一届大型消费电子产品展览会-世界移动通信大会-经过多番努力最终被取消。
  CTA（CES背后的管理机构）似乎已经在计划今年展会的缩小版亲自表演，此前夏季，位于柏林的IFA背后的团队也采取了类似的行动。但是，到了7月，显然这一计划是站不住脚的。坦率地说，在遏制这种病毒方面，美国并没有全力以赴（如果我不承认我们在撰写本文的那一天仅造成40万人死亡，我将被解雇。 ）。
 CES 2021远非过去一年来首次虚拟化的技术展览会。另一方面，事件的规模和范围在这里相对独特。根据CTA，2020年的展会吸引了170,000名与会者。过去一年中，我参加的大多数技术活动都是由一家公司举办的。 CES显然显然是完全不同的野兽。
 CTA在行业中的角色（nee CEA）无疑给了它一定的商誉。毕竟，演出可以追溯到60年代后期。多年来，它一直在消退和流动（受到诸如2008年金融危机之类的外部力量的打击），但一直保持不变。我们中那些已经这样做了一段时间的人倾向于面对节目时充满期待和恐惧。但是公司总是出来的。
  根据CTA的数字，在2021年的活动中，将近2,000家公司发布了产品。与去年参展的4,419家公司相比，这一数字相形见pale，但这是可以预期的。除了活动的不确定性之外，对于许多公司而言，这是糟糕的一年。我当然有我的疑问和疑惑-其中最主要的是对于初创公司而言，像这样的事件的价值是什么？没有面对面的元素，难道这不是又一次迷失在噪音中的机会吗？ 
我听到了来自初创公司的类似反馈，尽管最终有将近700家公司选择参加展会。我知道，因为为了我们的报道目的，我最终仔细检查了所有这些内容。这让我回想起当年挑战自己在展览的每平方英寸中走动的那一年的内心记忆，最终由于完全不同的原因而变得充满挑战。
 最终，这是我最想念的元素。对我而言，CES的最大吸引力在于发现的元素。尤里卡公园（Eureka Park）是金沙博览会（Sands Expo）展会上拥挤的起步部分，很容易成为最好的部分。绝大多数参展商都不适合我们，但我仍然要为以前从未见过的新事物和创新而绊脚石。呆在里面的博主本能不断涌现，我迫不及待想回到笔记本电脑前向世界讲述。
 今年没有尤里卡公园-甚至没有虚拟版本。只是没有一种很好的方法可以在线估计一个展示厅，至少我不知道。一些现有的联系人提供给我邮件中的内容以进行查找。例如，Sensel有一个新版本的触控板（今天宣布，它将集成到联想最新的ThinkPad中）。但是出于明显的原因，不可能让所有700家创业公司都将评论单元发送到我在皇后区的一居室中。
 虚拟事件最重要的是，突出了这种规模的事件的技术局限性。新闻发布会非常简单（尽管我发现CTA所采用的各种平台令人沮丧）。对于参展公司来说，这些广告常常感觉像是冗长的广告。当然，也有面对面的版本，但我们往往对这种视觉视而不见。就我个人而言，没有什么事情是新闻稿无法更有效地完成的。
 今年新闻发布的性质更加模糊。似乎更多的公司似乎在展会开始前就放弃了新闻，从而获得了自由。其他公司提供了自己的计数器编程。就我自己而言，这些事件的最大优势之一就是它们如何调节新闻流。我知道今年已经过去了，今年年初将有一个令人毛骨悚然的艰难星期，其中将发布大量新闻。
 由于今年的CES不再是重心，因此我预计新闻流的细分程度会降低。在过去的几年中，我曾向同事们评论说，关于硬件新闻，“再也没有缓慢的季节了”，这只会增加这种情绪。显然，让事情更均匀地传播是有好处的，但是我有一种感觉，我们正在朝着类似于一年四季一系列类似CES的小型活动的方向发展，这种想法使我的血液变得冷酷。
  近年来很明显，公司愿意跟随CES的步伐摆脱CES的喧嚣，转而支持自己的活动。虚拟事件是采用该方法的绝佳机会。与此同时，苹果公司在年底将每月从一个活动转移到一系列较小的活动。如果您不要求人们飞越全国或世界各地参加活动，那么符合新闻资格的标准就会大大降低。也许不是让成千上万的公司在一场活动中争夺我们的注意力，我们正在转向一种模式，其中有数千个事件。头脑陷入僵局。 
我对CTA的格式有一些过分具体的抱怨，但我会将它们保存在事后调查中，以便我填写或不填写。 我仍然在虚拟事件中发现价值。 与很多我不熟悉的创业公司交谈是一个借口。 但是，最终，我认为该事件证明了这样一个事实，即尽管我们感叹像CES这样的事件所带来的所有头痛和感冒，但面对面还是有很多价值 事件。  毫无疑问，CTA和其他此类组织正在争先恐后地重返现场活动，即使隆重的疫苗推出给预期的时间表留下了很大的问号。 我们很有可能将2020/2021视为面对面贸易展览会结束的开始。 但是，鉴于我们在过去一年中遇到的种种限制，我不准备在不久的将来宣布它们完全失效。
note_en:
I’ve always assumed that if trade shows were to go extinct, it would be a gradual process — a slow fade into cultural irrelevance, like bookstores and record stores (both things I miss dearly). Technology has, for many intents and purposes, dramatically reduced their relative value to our society.
 While it’s undoubtedly true that Spotify and the Kindle Store are lacking in much of the appeal and all of the charm of their real-world counterparts, we’re happy to sacrifice all that and more at the alter of convenience.
  A rampaging pandemic has effectively given us a year without in-person trade shows. That means, among other things, we’ve had a much more immediate control variable in this question about trade shows. Last year’s CES managed to get in just under the wire. The next major consumer electronics show — Mobile World Congress — was eventually canceled after much hand-wringing.
  The CTA (the governing body behind CES) appeared to have been planning a scaled-back in-person version of the show this year, following a similar move by the team behind the Berlin-based IFA over the summer. By July, however, it was clear that such a plan was untenable. To put it bluntly, the United States didn’t have its shit together when it comes to keeping this virus in check (I’d be remiss if I didn’t acknowledge that we just hit 400,000 deaths on the day I’m writing this).
 CES 2021 was far from the first tech show to go all virtual over this past year. The size and scope of the event, on the other hand, are relatively unique here. Per the CTA, the 2020 show drew north of 170,000 attendees. The majority of the tech events I’ve attended virtually in the past year have been put on by a single company. CES is obviously a different beast entirely.
 The CTA’s (nee CEA) role in the industry certainly afforded it a fair bit of goodwill up front. The show, after all, dates back to the late-60s. It has ebbed and flowed over the years (taking hits from external forces like the 2008 financial crisis), but it has remained a constant. Those of us who’ve been doing this for a while tend to face the show with equal parts anticipation and dread. But the companies always come out.
  Per the CTA’s numbers, nearly 2,000 companies launched products at the 2021 event. The figure pales in comparison to the 4,419 companies exhibiting last year, but that’s to be expected. In addition to the uncertain nature of the event, it’s been a remarkably crappy year for plenty of companies. I certainly had my questions and doubts going in — chief among them was the value of an event like this for a startup? Without an in-person element, wasn’t this just yet another chance to get lost in the noise?
 I heard similar feedback from startups on the side, though ultimately nearly 700 chose to exhibit at the show. I know because I ended up going through all of them for the purposes of our coverage. It brought back a kind of visceral memory of the year I challenged myself to walk every square inch of the show, and ended up being challenging for entirely different reasons.
 Ultimately, this was the element I missed the most. For me, CES’s biggest appeal has been the element of discovery. Eureka Park, the jam-packed startup portion of the show at the Sands Expo, is easily the best part. The vast majority of exhibitors are not for us, but I still get a charge stumbling on something new and innovative I’ve not seen before. The blogger instinct that lives dormant inside kicks in and I can’t wait to get back in front of my laptop to tell the world.
 There was no Eureka Park this year — not even a virtual version. There’s just no good way to approximate a show floor online — at least none that I’m aware of. A couple of existing contacts offered to send me stuff in the mail to look out. Sensel, for instance, has a new version of its trackpad (which it announced today will be integrated into Lenovo’s latest ThinkPad). But for obvious reasons, it’s just not possible to get all 700 startups to send review units to my one-bedroom in Queens.
 More than anything, the virtual event highlighted the technology limitations of an event at this scale. Press conferences are simple enough (though I found frustration in the various different platforms the CTA employed). More often than not, these felt like lengthy commercials for the exhibiting company. The in-person versions are, as well, of course, but we tend to be blinded by the spectacle. For my own purposes, there just wasn’t a lot that that couldn’t have been accomplished more efficiently with a press release.
 The nature of news releases was far more nebulous this year. More companies seemingly took liberties by dumping their news well ahead of the show. Other companies offered their own sort of counter programming. One of the biggest advantages to these events when it comes to my own peace of mind is how they regulate the news flow. I know going into the year that there’s going to be one hair-pullingly difficult week at the beginning of the year where a ton of news is announced.
 With CES less of a center of gravity this year, I anticipated seeing a less segmented news flow. I’ve commented to colleagues over the last couple of years that there’s “no more slow season” when it comes to hardware news, and this will likely only increase that sentiment. Obviously there’s upside in having things more evenly spread out, but I’ve got the feeling we’re moving toward something more akin to a series of small CES-like events throughout the year, and the thought makes my blood turn cold.
  It’s been clear in recent years that companies would rather break out from the noise of CES in favor of their own events, following in Apple’s footsteps. Virtual events are a perfect opportunity to adopt that approach. Apple, meanwhile, moved from one event to a series of one smaller event every month toward the end of the year. When you’re not asking people to fly across the country or world to attend an event, the bar for what qualifies as news lowers considerably. Perhaps instead of having thousands of companies vying for our attention at one event, we’re moving toward a model in which there are instead thousands of events. The mind boggles.
 I have some hyper-specific grievances about the CTA’s format, but I’ll save them for the post-event survey that I may or may not get around to filling out. I still found value in the virtual event. It was an excuse to talk to a bunch of startups I wasn’t familiar with. Ultimately, however, I think the event served as a testament to the fact that as much as we bemoan all of the headaches and head colds that come with an event like CES, there’s still a lot of value to be had in the in-person event.
 There’s little doubt that the CTA and the rest of these sorts of organizations are champing at the bit to return to in-person events, even as a bumpy vaccine rollout leaves a big question mark around the expected timeline. There’s a very good chance that we’ll view 2020/2021 as the beginning of the end for the in-person trade show. But given the sorts of limitations we’ve seen in the past year, I’m not ready to declare them fully dead any time soon.
title: 关于首个全虚拟CES的思考
title_en: Reflections on the first all-virtual CES

Instagram主管Adam Mosseri对该平台的TikTok竞争对手Reels并不感到兴奋。在今天接受The Verge的Decoder播客采访时，他讨论了8月份在美国推出的格式。
 他说：“我对此还不满意。” “我们在分享人数和消费人数方面都在增长，但我们还有很长的路要走。而且我们必须诚实地说TikTok领先。”
 他接着说，团队目前主要专注于“桌上赌注”，而不是专注于如何使自己与众不同。具体来说，他呼唤TikTok的创新创意工具，例如滤镜和特效，它们激发了新的视频模因格式，因为Instagram尚未自行制作，但最终需要这么做。
  “我认为我们必须变得更好，才能开发出功能强大且富有创造力的工具，这些工具不一定是模因或一瞬间，而是要赋予比我们更具创造力并为生活谋生的人们创造能力他说。
 Instagram还发现其许多不同的视频格式之间存在冲突。莫塞里说，“大多数人”可能不知道发布到Instagram和IGTV的视频之间的区别，这是自2018年以来存在的较长视频的应用程序格式。
  “这可能是一种微妙的区分，以至于无法引起任何人的共鸣，所以我们正在考虑如何（不仅可以使用IGTV，而且可以在整个Instagram上）简化和合并想法，因为去年我们投入了很多新赌注，”他说。 “我认为今年我们必须回到对简单性和工艺的关注上。”
 这与Instagram推出该格式并将其称为“视频的未来”时截然不同。但这证明了TikTok的迅速成功，它在2018年尚未在主流文化中广为人知，但现在已成为对话，模因和病毒式舞蹈的驱动力。 Mosseri暗示IGTV和Reels以及发布到Instagram网格上的常规视频需要某种形式的合并和清晰度，这是有道理的，尤其是在该应用尝试同时完成所有操作时。
note_en:
Adam Mosseri, the head of Instagram, isn’t thrilled with the platform’s TikTok competitor  Reels. He discussed the format, which launched in the US in August, during an interview with   The Verge’s  Decoder podcast today.
 “I’m not yet happy with it,” he says. “We’re growing both in terms of how much people are sharing and how much people are consuming, but we have a long way to go. And we have to be honest that TikTok is ahead.”
 He went on to say the team is mostly focused on “table stakes” right now, and not honed in on how to differentiate itself. Specifically, he calls out TikTok’s innovative creative tools, like filters and effects that spur new video meme formats as something Instagram isn’t making on its own yet but needs to eventually do.
  “I think we have to get better at building more powerful and creative tools that aren’t necessarily a meme or a sort of moment in a package, but give people who are more creative than us and make content for a living the ability to make something that’s going to pop,” he says.
 Instagram is also seeing a conflict between its many different video formats. Mosseri says “most people” likely don’t know the difference between videos posted to Instagram and IGTV, the app’s format for longer videos that’s existed  since 2018.
  “That’s probably too nuanced a distinction to resonate with anybody, so we’re looking about how we can — not just with IGTV, but across all of Instagram — simplify and consolidate ideas, because last year we placed a lot of new bets,” he says. “I think this year we have to go back to our focus on simplicity and craft.”
 This is a wildly different tone than when Instagram launched the format and called it the “ future of video.” But it speaks to the rapid success of TikTok which, in 2018, wasn’t fully known in mainstream culture but is now a driver of conversation, memes, and viral dances. Mosseri hints that IGTV and Reels, as well as general videos posted to the Instagram grid, need some sort of consolidation and clarity, which makes sense, especially when the app is trying to do everything all at once.
title: Instagram负责人说他对转盘还不满意，可能会“整合”视频格式
title_en: Instagram Lead Says He's Not Happy With Reels Yet, Might 'Consolidate' Video Formats

Netflix正式吸引了2亿订户，但该公司并不打算固步自封。
    随着Netflix与Disney +和HBO Max之类的服务继续竞争，Netflix计划继续改善其用户体验，以使观众回头。去年，Netflix推出了“值得期待”，“前10名”和“受欢迎的新产品”部分，以帮助发现。Netflix表示，它也准备推出另一项新功能。在今年上半年，该服务将添加一个工具，使Netflix可以根据用户的偏好来选择标题，而不是要求他们浏览要观看的东西。
 “我们的会员基本上可以向我们表明，他们希望完全跳过浏览，单击一个按钮，然后我们会为他们挑选一个标题，以便立即播放。在这种情况下，这是一种非常有效的机制，对会员非常有用。”首席运营官兼首席产品官格雷格·彼得斯（Greg Peters）在收益问答中表示。当被问及该服务是否称该工具为“我感到很幸运”时，彼得斯表示，该工具在发布时会被称为“别的东西”。
  我们已与Netflix联系，以获取有关标题建议的更多信息，并在我们回听时会更新此信息。
 Netflix在周二发布的第四季度股东信函中表示，它设法获得了850万付费订阅，以帮助这家流媒体巨头突破2亿大关。总而言之，该公司在2020年期间总共增加了3,700万付费订阅，这无疑是由于冠状病毒大流行期间锁定订单和隔离措施导致电视收看和流媒体激增所致。 Netflix表示，在某些情况下，会员数量比去年第四季度增长了23％。
   随着众多新流媒体服务争夺消费者的钱和收看时间，Netflix的竞争比以往任何时候都要激烈。但该公司表示，人们期待这种新的竞争“持续很多年”，其中​​包括来自迪士尼和华纳媒体（WarnerMedia）等重量级企业。做出回应的关键是令人振奋的原创内容流失，以及最新的成功，例如《 The Crown》，《 Barbarians》和《 Selena：The Series》。
 随着在国外市场发行国际电影，其内容战略也得到了加强。该公司特别引用了法国抢劫电影卢平（Lupin）的一个例子，该电影获得了不了名。在美国前十名中排名第二。在2020年，该公司还发布了一些非常成功的原创作品，这些原创作品享有很高的声望和文化影响力。该公司表示，老虎金，皇后甘比特和布里奇顿都是三本垒打。 
尽管大流行大大推迟了生产时间和发行日期，但Netflix表示，它仍有望在2021年的每个星期发行至少一部原创电影。此外，该公司表示，后期制作或准备就绪的影片多达500多种 达到其服务。  不过，最令人惊讶的是Netflix声称它“非常接近”现金流为正，并且不再需要筹集外部资金。 此外，该公司计划“在到期时用手头现金偿还债券，因为我们目前远远超过了最低现金需求。” 有关这方面的更多信息可能会在今天晚些时候的投资者电话会议中分享。  总而言之，对于Netflix来说，这是令人难以置信的一年。 看起来，尽管有众多想要争夺桂冠的竞争对手，但Netflix仍然是流媒体领域的王者-无论如何，到目前为止。  帖子已更新，以添加来自Netflix的电话会议的其他信息。
note_en:
Netflix has officially amassed 200 million subscribers, but the company isn’t planning to rest on its laurels.
    As it continues to compete with services like Disney+ and HBO Max, Netflix plans to continue to improve its user experience to keep viewers returning. It introduced its “worth the wait,” “top 10,” and “new and popular” sections last year to help with discovery, and Netflix says it’s preparing to roll out another new feature as well. In the first half of this year, the service will add a tool to allow Netflix to pick a title based on a user’s preferences rather than requiring them to browse for something to watch.
 “Our members can basically indicate to us that they want to skip browsing entirely, click one button, and we’ll pick a title for them just to instantly play. And that’s a great mechanism that’s worked quite for members in that situation,” COO and chief product officer Greg Peters said during an   earnings Q&A. Asked whether the service was going to call the tool “I’m feeling lucky,” Peters said it would be called something different at launch.
  We’ve reached out to Netflix for more information about how titles are suggested and will update this post when we hear back.
 Netflix said in its fourth-quarter   shareholders letter released Tuesday that it managed to secure 8.5 million more paid subscriptions to help the streaming giant cross the 200 million mark. All told, the company added a total of 37 million paid subscriptions during 2020, no doubt helped by the surge in TV viewing and streaming that resulted from lockdown orders and quarantine measures amid the coronavirus pandemic. For some context, Netflix says memberships were up 23 percent from the previous year in the fourth quarter.
   With the arrival of so many new streaming services competing for consumer money and viewing time, Netflix has more competition than ever before. But the company says it’s been expecting this new competition—including from heavyweights like Disney and WarnerMedia—for “many years.” Key to its response is its head-spinning churn of original content and latest successes like the most recent season of  The Crown,  Barbarians, and  Selena: The Series.
 Its content strategy is also boosted with the release of its international films in outside markets. The company specifically cited an example of the French heist film  Lupin, which enjoyed a no. 2 spot in its U.S. top 10 list. In 2020, the company also released a number of highly successful originals during the year that enjoyed critical claim and cultural impact.  Tiger King,  Queen’s Gambit, and  Bridgerton were three such home runs, the company said.
  Even with the pandemic significantly delaying production timelines and release dates, Netflix says it’s still on track to release at least one original film   every week of 2021. Plus, the company said it has a staggering 500-plus titles either in post-production or ready to hit its service.
 Most surprising, though, was Netflix’s claim that it is “very close” to being cash-flow positive and no longer needs to raise external financing. Additionally, the company plans “on repaying the bond at maturity out of cash on hand, as we are currently well above our minimum cash needs.” More information on this front will likely be shared during its investors call later today.
  All in all, it’s been an incredible year for Netflix. And it seems that in spite of the number of competitors who’d like to swipe its crown, Netflix remains king in the streaming space—for now, anyway.
  This post has been updated to add additional information from Netflix’s earnings call.
title: Netflix最终在吸引2亿订户的同时增加了流轮盘赌功能
title_en: Netflix Is Finally Adding a Streaming Roulette Feature As It Clinches 200 Million Subscribers

   鉴于加里·根斯勒（Gary Gensler）对这项技术的持续和深切兴趣，提名他成为加密货币行业的喜讯。
 为何重要：尽管SEC通过特朗普时代主席杰伊·克莱顿（Jay Clayton）执政下的执法行动等表达了一些观点，但该行业一直渴望获得更加清晰的监管。
 他们在说什么：“他是一个非常聪明和体贴的人，并且在过去的几年中，他一直沉迷于加密技术，因此他对生态系统，技术和业务也非常了解。”著名数字货币智囊团Coin Center的执行董事杰里·布里托（Jerry Brito）说。
 倒叙：根斯勒（Gensler）是前银行家，曾在美国财政部任职，并领导商品和期货交易委员会。他于2018年加入麻省理工学院（MIT）教授有关区块链技术的课程并作为媒体实验室的数字货币计划的顾问而成为头条新闻。
 他在2018年的演讲中正确预测，与比特币不同，数字令牌Ether和XRP表现出证券的特征，尽管情况不同。 6月，SEC公司财务总监威廉·欣曼（William Hinman）宣布，鉴于以太坊目前分散的网络，今天它已不是证券，尽管他暗示其最初发行可能是出售证券。 
就在克莱顿（Clayton）于12月离任前几天，美国证券交易委员会（SEC）起诉了RXP创作者瑞普（Ripple）涉嫌出售未注册证券。
 是的，但是：尽管他对加密货币感兴趣并很欣赏，但Gensler的任期并不意味着该行业是万能的。
 Brito说：“他非常致力于证券法并执行它们。” ＆＃34;我认为人们对我们的证券法有疑问，他们将其与SEC的问题混为一谈。
 总体情况：Gensler将领导一个对加密货币并不陌生的委员会，甚至在最近将其金融技术（和加密货币）部门设立为独立办公室时，其董事现在将直接向董事长汇报。
 专员海斯特·皮尔斯（Hester Peirce）去年获得了又一个五年任期，她对该行业也非常友好-她被许多人亲切地称为“加密妈妈”。
 下一步是什么：除了继续表达其在代币产品方面的立场，该行业还将渴望在监管方面获得更多的透明度，包括代币交易所（尤其是所谓的“分布式交易所”）和对数字货币的托管。
  底线是：Gensler可能处于进一步缩小加密行业与证券法之间差距的良好位置，但是作为SEC主席，他还必须将时间花在委员会职权范围内的其他问题上。

Make your busy days simpler with Axios AM/PM. Catch up on what's new and why it matters in just 5 minutes.
 
   Gary Gensler’s nomination to head the U.S. Securities and Exchange is welcome news for the cryptocurrency industry given his ongoing and deep interest in the technology.
 Why it matters: Although the SEC has conveyed some of its views via enforcement actions and the like under Trump-era chair Jay Clayton, the industry has been yearning for even more regulatory clarity.
 What they’re saying: “He is a very smart and thoughtful person, and for the past few years, he has steeped himself in crypto, so he is very, very knowledgeable about the ecosystem and the technology, and the business too,” says Jerry Brito, executive director of Coin Center, a prominent digital currency think tank.
 Flashback: Gensler, a former banker who has served in the Treasury Department and headed the Commodities and Futures Trading Commission, made  headlines in 2018 when he joined MIT to teach courses about blockchain technology and as an adviser to the Media Lab’s Digital Currency Initiative.
 He correctly predicted  in a 2018 speech that unlike Bitcoin, digital tokens Ether and XRP exhibit characteristics of securities, though their cases are different. In June, SEC director of Corporate Finance William Hinman  declared that Ether is not a security  today given its now-widely decentralized network, though he hinted its initial issuance was likely a sale of securities.
 Just days before Clayton left office in December,  the SEC filed a lawsuit against RXP creator Ripple for allegedly selling unregistered securities.
 Yes, but: Despite his interest and appreciation for cryptocurrencies, Gensler’s tenure won’t mean an industry free-for-all.
 “He’s very committed to securities laws and enforcing them,” says Brito. "I think people have problems with our securities laws, and they confuse that with having a problem with the SEC."
 The big picture: Gensler will be leading a commission that's already not a stranger to crypto, even recently setting up its financial tech (and cryptocurrency) unit as a standalone office whose director will now report directly to the chairman.
 Commissioner Hester Peirce, who was confirmed last year for another five-year term, has also been quite friendly to the industry — she’s been affectionately nicknamed “crypto mom” by many.
 What’s next: Beyond continuing to telegraph its position on token offerings, other areas the industry will be eager to get more regulatory clarity on include token exchanges (especially so-called "distributed exchanges") and custody for cryptocurrencies.
  The bottom line: Gensler may be in a good position to further bridge the gap between the crypto industry and securities laws, but as SEC chair he’ll also have to devote his time to other issues under the commission’s purview.
title: 拜登挑选前CFTC主席加里·根斯勒（Gary Gensler）担任SEC负责人，他曾在麻省理工学院教授有关加密货币和区块链的课程
title_en: Biden picks former CFTC chairman Gary Gensler, who taught courses on cryptocurrencies and blockchain at MIT, to head the SEC
